text: Hi friends, here I am going to show you a laparoscopic cholecystectomy procedure which is done today.
The gallbladder is visible at the base of the liver, showing its anatomical position in relation to the liver.
The surgeon performs dissection within Calot's triangle, carefully separating anatomical structures in this region during the laparoscopic cholecystectomy.
The surgeon separates the cystic duct and cystic artery within the Calot's triangle, ensuring careful dissection in this critical area.
During the four-port laparoscopic cholecystectomy, the surgeon successfully separates the cystic duct and prepares to apply clips for ligation.
The surgeon applies three clips to the cystic duct, with two clips remaining inside the body and one intended to be removed with the gallbladder during its extraction from the liver bed.
The surgeon creates space around the cystic artery within the Calot's triangle and applies clips to secure the artery, visible adjacent to the gallbladder at the liver base.
The surgeon clips the cystic artery within the dissected Calot's triangle, securing the vessel with surgical clips.
The surgeon identifies a visible lymph node in the surgical field during the laparoscopic cholecystectomy.
The surgeon applies the first clip to the lymph node during the laparoscopic cholecystectomy procedure.
The surgeon points out a white structure above the lymph node, identifying it as the lymph node itself.
The surgeon cuts the cystic duct and proceeds to dissect the gallbladder from the cystic plate, separating it from the liver bed.
The surgeon removes the cystic artery following its identification and dissection in the previous steps.
A slight black leakage is visible in the surgical field, possibly indicating bleeding or bile leakage during the cholecystectomy procedure.
The surgeon uses a Hickory instrument to dissect the gallbladder from the cystic plate, continuing the separation of tissue layers.

The surgeon removes the gallbladder from the surgical field using ovum forceps. Stones are extracted from the gallbladder, and the specimen is removed through the port. The area is irrigated, a drain is placed, and the pneumoperitoneum is deflated.
The surgeon stretches back the alpha port incisions, adjusting the surgical access points.
Thank you for giving your valuable time.
